Safety Mar 27, 2026

Three killed in Hawaii tour helicopter crash on Kauai

Three people died and two others were injured after an Airborne Aviation tour helicopter crashed on the afternoon…...

Tour helicopter operations in Hawaii continue facing safety scrutiny as accident rates remain disproportionately high compared to other aviation sectors.

Commercial Mar 27, 2026

SES meoSphere will be core to new MEO/LEO inflight connectivity

WASHINGTON D.C. — Rapidly putting the wheels in motion to launch a next-generation Medium Earth Orbit satellite network, dubbed meoSphere, SES intends to pair the new service with Low Earth Orbit satcom to power multi-orbit MEO/LEO inflight connectivity for airlines, company CEO Adel Al-Saleh...

SES's dual MEO/LEO satellite architecture will enable airlines to access redundant, low-latency connectivity by leveraging complementary orbital characteristics for improved coverage and reliability.

See Runway Girl Network →

Drones Mar 27, 2026

This firmware update makes Avata 360 ready for real flying

The new DJI Avata 360 drone is arriving with a feature-rich day-one firmware update that significantly expands what this immersive FPV drone can do right out of the box. The initial firmware rollout, headlined by v01.00.0100 for the aircraft, arrives alongside updates for the entire ecosystem,...

DJI's day-one firmware enables full operational capability for the Avata 360, reducing post-purchase configuration and accelerating user readiness for commercial and recreational FPV operations.

See DroneDJ →

PAI
Safety Mar 27, 2026

The Evolution of Aviation SMS

From the Wright brothers to the 2024 FAA rule — how aviation safety evolved from reactive investigation to proactive risk management.

Modern SMS shifts aviation from investigating accidents after they occur to systematically identifying and mitigating hazards before they cause incidents, fundamentally changing how operators prioritize safety investments.

See PAI Consulting — SMS Quick Takes →

The World’s Largest Air Forces By Number Of Pilots

Which air force has the deepest roster?

Pilot inventory depth correlates with sustained operational readiness and global power projection capability, making force size a critical metric beyond aircraft quantity.

Aerospace Mar 27, 2026

India’s Bellatrix raises $20 million following overseas expansion drive

India-based Bellatrix Aerospace announced March 27 it has raised $20 million to ramp up production of its satellite propulsion systems after securing its first large commercial customer outside the country.

Indian space propulsion startups are now competing for international commercial customers, signaling growing aerospace manufacturing capabilities outside traditional Western suppliers.

See SpaceNews →

Aerospace Mar 27, 2026

NextSTEP-3 E: Network Extension for User Continuity and Sustainability (NEXUS) Ka-Band Backward-Compatible Relay Broad

Synopsis | 03/23/26 https://sam.gov/workspace/contract/opp/e33cd0cc61064a6497a55fca8e9b30c6/view NASA intends to release a BAA under Next Space Technologies for Exploration Partnerships (NextSTEP-3), Appendix E, for Project NEXUS, Ka-band Backward- Compatible Relay. As the aging Tracking and Data...

NASA's NEXUS project modernizes aging spacecraft communication infrastructure through backward-compatible Ka-band relay systems, enabling seamless transitions for existing missions while supporting future exploration requirements.

See NASA →

The 5 Highest Altitude Reconnaissance Aircraft Currently Operated By The United States

These aircraft offer long-range capabilities, a factor that pairs well with their high-altitude flying.

High-altitude reconnaissance aircraft's extended range enables persistent surveillance coverage across vast geographic areas, multiplying their strategic value beyond altitude alone.

Safety Culture in Aviation SMS

How organizational culture shapes safety outcomes and what it takes to build a reporting culture that supports SMS.

SMS effectiveness depends less on procedures than on psychological safety, where employees trust that reporting hazards won't trigger blame.

Drones Mar 27, 2026

DJI Fly update adds support for new Avata 360 drone

The DJI Fly app has officially been updated to version 1.20.0, adding support for the newly launched Avata 360 drone. The update also confirms Avata 360’s compatibility with the DJI RC 2, DJI Goggles 3, and DJI Goggles N3. more…...

DJI's ecosystem expansion enables the Avata 360 to leverage existing hardware platforms, reducing operator investment barriers for advanced FPV drone operations.

See DroneDJ →

Commercial Mar 27, 2026

The $2B blind spot: Why ‘good enough’ is killing airline profitability

AeroTime columnist Ann Cederhall is an instructor with IATA on Airline Distribution Strategy and with Aeroclass on Airline Retailing, Ann is a frequent speaker and…...

Airlines' acceptance of suboptimal distribution and retailing practices costs $2B annually, suggesting that strategic investment in ancillary revenue optimization directly impacts bottom-line profitability more than capacity growth.

See AeroTime Hub →

General Aviation Mar 27, 2026

Tennessee Airport Boards Face Possible State Takeover

Senate panel advances measure to replace existing airport authority boards with state appointees.

State consolidation of airport governance could centralize strategic planning and capital allocation but may reduce local community input on facility development priorities.

See AVweb →

Aerospace Mar 27, 2026

Artemis II Crew’s Suits

The Orion Crew Survival System suits that Commander Reid Wiseman, Pilot Victor Glover, and Mission Specialist Christina Koch from NASA, and Mission Specialist Jeremy Hansen from the CSA (Canadian Space Agency) will wear on the Artemis II test flight are seen in the suit-up room of the Neil A....

Advanced spacesuit technology developed for lunar missions demonstrates cross-agency collaboration that could influence next-generation aerospace pressure garment systems.

Safety Mar 27, 2026

Flying in the Unforgiving Wire Environment (The Rotorcraft Collective)

Many pilots mistakenly believe they will have sufficient reaction time if they encounter power lines and other wires. Yet wire strikes have been among the top three causes of fatal helicopter...

Wire strike fatalities persist despite pilot overconfidence in reaction time, requiring systemic detection or avoidance technology rather than relying on human reflexes.

Safety Mar 27, 2026

EASA Warns Of Stolen Aircraft Parts Threatening Global Flights

The agency has been alerted to a compromised shipment of more than 600 parts from four engine families.

Stolen aircraft engine parts now circulating in global supply chains pose verification challenges that could compromise airworthiness across multiple engine manufacturers simultaneously.

General Aviation Mar 27, 2026

From the Flight Deck – Eastern Iowa Airport, IA (CID)

Eastern Iowa Airport (CID) is a medium-sized multi-use airport located south of Cedar Rapids, Iowa. Traffic includes basic training aircraft up through air carrier aircraft. Flight schools often use...

Regional airports like CID serve as vital training hubs that develop pilot pipelines while simultaneously handling commercial traffic, creating operational complexity that requires careful traffic management.

Northrop Grumman, US Air Force begin Sentinel ICBM silo prototyping in Utah

The US Air Force and Northrop Grumman broke ground on a prototype launch silo for the LGM-35A Sentinel…...

Silo prototyping accelerates Sentinel's 2030 operational readiness timeline while testing next-generation ICBM basing concepts before full-scale deployment.

General Aviation Mar 27, 2026

Flight School Closure Leaves Students Seeking Refunds

Students and another Missouri flight school dispute how unused training balances will be handled.

Flight school closures highlight the absence of industry-standard consumer protection mechanisms for pre-paid aviation training, creating potential financial and career disruptions for aspiring pilots.

Commercial Mar 27, 2026

Surprise! The US Airport With The World's Rarest Airbus A380 Service

The major US hub will have just 89 A380 departures this year.

Limited A380 service concentration at a single US hub signals structural demand challenges for ultra-large aircraft in domestic markets despite operational capabilities.

Regulatory Mar 27, 2026

Boeing's 737 MAX 10 Certification: The Unseen Human Effort To Restore Trust

Testing the uncertified aircraft involves a huge team that stretches way beyond the pilots flying the aircraft.

Certification requires coordinated efforts across engineering, quality, and regulatory teams whose behind-the-scenes work determines public confidence in aircraft safety.

See Simple Flying →

Industry Mar 27, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization across integrated airline-airport-airspace systems offers near-term emissions reduction opportunities without waiting for next-generation aircraft technology.

See MDPI →

Safety Mar 27, 2026

Is Turbulence Actually Dangerous?

The phenomenon is extremely common.

Modern aircraft are engineered to withstand turbulence far exceeding what occurs naturally, making structural failure from turbulence virtually impossible despite passenger discomfort.

Video: F-18 Intercept Near Iran & Atlantic Engine Trouble with Sam Rutherford

Ferry pilots operating single-engine aircraft over oceanic routes face compounded risk factors including extended engine-out scenarios and potential military encounters in geopolitically sensitive airspace.

Press Release: AIX on what’s redefining cabin innovation across APAC

Aircraft Interiors Expo (AIX) has revealed critical new insights into what’s reshaping aircraft cabin innovation across the Asia Pacific (APAC) region, and how airlines and suppliers must adapt in one of the world’s fastest evolving aviation markets. Drawing on insight from airlines, design...

Asia Pacific airlines are prioritizing passenger experience investments and sustainable cabin technologies, creating supply chain opportunities for manufacturers willing to localize production.

Drones Mar 27, 2026

The ‘ground truth’ gap in AgTech: Why satellites alone can’t save supply chains

The Earth observation sector is experiencing a golden age. Satellite hardware and launch costs have plummeted, and computer power, driven by AI and analytics, continues to improve. That’s led to a genuine revolution in precision agriculture and automated supply chain monitoring. For agribusinesses,...

Satellite-based agricultural monitoring requires ground-level validation to prevent costly supply chain errors, creating demand for integrated drone and sensor networks in precision agriculture operations.

See SpaceNews →

Safety Mar 27, 2026

La Guardia Crash: NTSB Releases Footage & Additional Info Regarding Tragedy

You are probably well-aware by now that an Air Canada Express Bombardier CRJ900 crashed into a fire truck at La Guardia airport on the night of March 22nd, 2026. While, on the surface, the tragic accident that took the lives of the two aircraft pilots looks to be a straightforward case of...

The NTSB's release of crash footage and evidence provides critical details that may reveal previously undisclosed factors beyond the initial fire truck collision assessment.

Aerospace Mar 27, 2026

Industry says proposed NASA changes to commercial space station plans create confusion

NASA’s proposed changes to its support of commercial space stations have created concern and confusion among companies developing them, the head of an industry organization warned.

Industry uncertainty over NASA's shifting commercial station support timelines could delay private space infrastructure investments critical to post-ISS operations.
